# qcthat

[qcthat](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/) is a quality control
framework for R packages. It has been developed for use in the `gsm`
family of packages, such as
[`gsm.core`](https://github.com/Gilead-BioStats/gsm.core).

The goal of [qcthat](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/) is to
produce qualification reports linking GitHub issues to evidence that
those issues have been implemented. These reports can be used as part of
a quality control and acceptance process for R packages, particularly
those used in regulated environments such as clinical trials.

## ‚öôÔ∏è Installation

You can install the development version of
[qcthat](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/) from
[GitHub](https://github.com/) with:

``` r
# install.packages("pak")
pak::pak("Gilead-BioStats/qcthat")
```

Learn more in
[`vignette("qcthat")`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/articles/qcthat.md).

## üìã Example Report Process

[`Action_qcthat()`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/Action_qcthat.md)
installs a GitHub action to generate QC reports. At its core, it uses
the functions
[`QCPackage()`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/QCPackage.md),
[`QCPR()`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/QCPR.md),
[`QCCompletedIssues()`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/QCCompletedIssues.md),
and
[`QCMilestones()`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/QCMilestones.md)
to generate a report like this:

‚úÖ A qcthat issue test matrix with 1 milestone, 19 issues, and 59 tests

``` R
‚îî‚îÄ‚ñà‚îÄMilestone: v1.0.0 (19 issues, 59 tests)
  ‚îú‚îÄ‚òëÔ∏è‚îÄTechnical Task 101: Switch `lglShowIgnoredLabels` default to TRUE
  ‚îÇ ‚îî‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄIgnored issues are shown by default (#101)
  ‚îú‚îÄ‚òëÔ∏è‚îÄFeature 80: Filter main qcthat report to only "closed (completed)"
  ‚îÇ ‚îî‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄQCCompletedIssues filters to completed issues (#80, #69)
  ‚îú‚îÄ‚òëÔ∏è‚îÄBug 77: GHA-generated report stability
  ‚îÇ ‚îî‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄReports generated via GHA include information about the issues (#77, #37)
  ‚îú‚îÄ‚òëÔ∏è‚îÄFeature 73: Add qcthis.yaml to a package
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄInstallAction calls InstallFile with expected parts (#73)
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄAction_qcthat targets the expected action (#55, #68, #69, #73, #88, #141, #157, #198)
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄqcthatPath constructs paths (#73)
  ‚îÇ ‚îî‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄInstallFile copies files as expected (#73)
  ‚îú‚îÄ‚òëÔ∏è‚îÄTechnical Task 72: Add qc report to triggering PR as comment
  ‚îÇ ‚îî‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄCommentReport generates the expected call (#99, #72)
  ‚îú‚îÄ‚òëÔ∏è‚îÄRequirement 69: Package QC Report Usability
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄAction_qcthat targets the expected action (#55, #68, #69, #73, #88, #141, #157, #198)
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄCan print without milestone info (#40, #69)
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄQCPackage wraps the core qcthat functions (#46, #69)
  ‚îÇ ‚îî‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄQCCompletedIssues filters to completed issues (#80, #69)
  ‚îú‚îÄ‚òëÔ∏è‚îÄRequirement 68: PR/Branch Report
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄAction_qcthat targets the expected action (#55, #68, #69, #73, #88, #141, #157, #198)
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄQCMergeGH filters to merge-associated issues (#68, #84)
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄQCMergeLocal filters to ref-specific issues (#68, #84)
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄQCPR filters to PR-related issues (#68, #84)
  ‚îÇ ‚îî‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄQCMilestones reports on specific milestones (#88, #68)
  ‚îú‚îÄ‚òëÔ∏è‚îÄFeature 67: Ignore issues with `qcthat-nocov` label
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄCompileIssueTestMatrix excludes issues in chrIgnoredLabels (#67)
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄExtractDisposition() helper counts test errors as failures (#67)
  ‚îÇ ‚îî‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄCan report ignored issue counts (#67, #81)
  ‚îú‚îÄ‚òëÔ∏è‚îÄFeature 55: GHA: Report of associated issues
  ‚îÇ ‚îî‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄAction_qcthat targets the expected action (#55, #68, #69, #73, #88, #141, #157, #198)
  ‚îú‚îÄ‚òëÔ∏è‚îÄFeature 46: Wrapper to run everything
  ‚îÇ ‚îî‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄQCPackage wraps the core qcthat functions (#46, #69)
  ‚îú‚îÄ‚òëÔ∏è‚îÄFeature 40: Print Without Milestones
  ‚îÇ ‚îî‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄCan print without milestone info (#40, #69)
  ‚îú‚îÄ‚òëÔ∏è‚îÄBug 96: Don't include ignored labels in `QCIssues()` warnings
  ‚îÇ ‚îî‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄQCIssues doesn't warn about ignored issues (#96)
  ‚îú‚îÄ‚òëÔ∏è‚îÄBug 95: Install qcthat as part of Action installation
  ‚îÇ ‚îî‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄqcthat is installed as part of the GHA (#95)
  ‚îú‚îÄ‚òëÔ∏è‚îÄFeature 90: Function to create qcthat-nocov label
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄCreateGHLabel reports success conditional on lglVerbose (#90)
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄCreateGHLabel throws an error if the API doesn't report the expected result (#90)
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄCreateGHLabel attempts to update existing label (#90)
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄMaybeUpdateGHLabel decides based on lglUpdate (#90)
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄUpdateGHLabel makes the expected call (#90)
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄUpdateGHLabel throws an error if the API doesn't report the expected result (#90)
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄEmptyLabelsDF returns the expected structure (#90)
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄEnframeGHLabels returns NULL for empty list (#90)
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄEnframeGHLabels converts raw labels to data frame (#90)
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄEnframeGHLabels adds hash to color codes (#90)
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄFetchGHLabelsRaw calls the correct API endpoint (#90)
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄFetchGHLabels returns empty data frame when no labels exist (#90)
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄFetchGHLabels returns data frame with labels (#90)
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄDefault helpers return expected values (#90)
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄSetupGHLabels creates missing labels (#90)
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄPrepareDFLabels normalizes correctly (#90)
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄHelper functions normalize strings correctly (#90)
  ‚îÇ ‚îî‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄValidateDFLabels checks for required columns (#90)
  ‚îú‚îÄ‚òëÔ∏è‚îÄFeature 88: Report by Milestone
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄAction_qcthat targets the expected action (#55, #68, #69, #73, #88, #141, #157, #198)
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄQCMilestones reports on specific milestones (#88, #68)
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄQCMilestones warns about unknown milestones (#88)
  ‚îÇ ‚îî‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄQCMilestones errors with no valid milestones (#88)
  ‚îú‚îÄ‚òëÔ∏è‚îÄFeature 86: Function to report on specific issues
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄQCIssues reports on specific issues (#86)
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄQCIssues warns about unknown issues (#86)
  ‚îÇ ‚îî‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄQCIssues errors with no valid issues (#86)
  ‚îú‚îÄ‚òëÔ∏è‚îÄFeature 85: Report Issue-Test Coverage in Footer
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄPrinting an IssueTestMatrix outputs a user-friendly tree (#31, #36, #60, #85)
  ‚îÇ ‚îî‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄMakeITRCoverageFooter deals with all cases (#85)
  ‚îú‚îÄ‚òëÔ∏è‚îÄFeature 84: Function(s) to filter report to issues associated with PR/branch
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄFetchMergeCommitSHAs returns unique, sorted SHAs (#84, #133)
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄFetchAllMergePRNumbers returns unique, sorted PR numbers (#84)
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄFetchAllMergePRNumbers returns empty vector for no matching PRs (#84)
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄFetchPRRefs returns source and target refs (#84, #133, #149)
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄFetchRepoPRs returns an empty df when no issues found (#84)
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄFetchRepoPRs returns a formatted df for real PRs (#84)
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄGuessPRNumber delegates to its sub-functions (#84)
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄGetGHAPRNumber returns NULL for bad arg (#84, #163)
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄGetGHAPRNumber extracts PR number from lGHEventPayload when available (#84, #163)
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄGetGHAPRNumber returns NULL for bad extracted PR number (#84, #163)
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄFetchRefPRNumber fetches PR number for a branch (#84, #132)
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄQCMergeGH filters to merge-associated issues (#68, #84)
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄQCMergeLocal filters to ref-specific issues (#68, #84)
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄFindKeywordIssues extracts issues that will be closed by commits (#84)
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄQCPR errors informatively for bad intPRNumber (#84)
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄQCPR filters to PR-related issues (#68, #84)
  ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄPrepareGQLQuery constructs a query (#84)
  ‚îÇ ‚îî‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄGQLWrapper wraps a query correctly (#84)
  ‚îî‚îÄ‚òëÔ∏è‚îÄRequirement 81: Report issue test coverage
    ‚îî‚îÄ‚úÖ‚îÄCan report ignored issue counts (#67, #81)
# Issue state: üì• = open, ‚òëÔ∏è = closed (completed), ‚õî = closed (won't fix)
# Test disposition: ‚úÖ = passed, ‚ùå = failed, üö´ = skipped
```

‚úÖ All tests passed

üü¢ All issues have at least one test

üôà 2 issues with label ‚Äúqcthat-nocov‚Äù were ignored

## üôã Contributing

Contributions are welcome! Creating a utility package that is
generalizable and extensible to all sorts of repository structures is
challenging, and your input is greatly appreciated.

Before submitting a pull request, make sure to file an
[issue](https://github.com/Gilead-BioStats/qcthat/issues), which should
generally fall under one of the following categories:

- Bugfix: something is broken.
- Feature: something is wanted or needed.
- QC: documentation or metadata is incorrect or missing.

New code should generally follow the [tidyverse style
guide](https://style.tidyverse.org/).

### Code of Conduct

Please note that the [qcthat](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/)
project is released with a [Contributor Code of
Conduct](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.html).
By contributing to this project, you agree to abide by its terms.

# Package index

## All functions

- [`Action_qcthat()`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/Action_qcthat.md)
  : Use a GitHub Action to manage qcthat
- [`AttachReleaseReports()`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/AttachReleaseReports.md)
  : Attach QC reports to a GitHub release
- [`CommentAllReports()`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/CommentAllReports.md)
  : Comment on a PR or issue with QC reports
- [`CommentIssue()`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/CommentIssue.md)
  : Comment on a GitHub Issue
- [`CommentReport()`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/CommentReport.md)
  : Comment on a PR or issue with a QC report
- [`CommentUAT()`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/CommentUAT.md)
  **\[experimental\]** : Comment on a PR or issue with a UAT report
- [`CompileIssueTestMatrix()`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/CompileIssueTestMatrix.md)
  : Create a nested tibble of issues and tests
- [`CompileTestResults()`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/CompileTestResults.md)
  : Extract information from testthat results
- [`DefaultIgnoreLabels()`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/DefaultIgnoreLabels.md)
  : Default labels to ignore
- [`DefaultIgnoreLabelsDF()`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/DefaultIgnoreLabelsDF.md)
  : Default ignored labels as a tibble
- [`ExpectUserAccepts()`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/ExpectUserAccepts.md)
  **\[experimental\]** : Does a user accept the feature?
- [`ExtractTestsFromFiles()`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/ExtractTestsFromFiles.md)
  **\[experimental\]** : Extract test information from test files
- [`FetchPRIssueNumbers()`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/FetchPRIssueNumbers.md)
  : Fetch all issues associated with a GitHub pull request
- [`FetchRefPRNumber()`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/FetchRefPRNumber.md)
  : Fetch the pull request number for a branch or other git ref
- [`FetchRepoIssueClosers()`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/FetchRepoIssueClosers.md)
  : Fetch repository issue closers
- [`FetchRepoIssues()`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/FetchRepoIssues.md)
  : Fetch repository issues
- [`FetchRepoPRs()`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/FetchRepoPRs.md)
  : Fetch repository pull requests
- [`GetGHOwner()`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/GetGHOwner.md)
  : Find the owner of the target repository
- [`GetGHRepo()`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/GetGHRepo.md)
  : Find the name of the target repository
- [`GuessIssueNumber()`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/GuessIssueNumber.md)
  : Guess the relevant issue number from the GitHub event
- [`GuessMilestones()`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/GuessMilestones.md)
  : Guess relevant milestone names from the GitHub event
- [`GuessPRNumber()`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/GuessPRNumber.md)
  : Guess the most relevant pull request number
- [`GuessReleaseID()`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/GuessReleaseID.md)
  : Guess the relevant release id from the GitHub event
- [`IsCheckingUAT()`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/IsCheckingUAT.md)
  : Detect whether the user is specifically checking UAT issues
- [`LoadGHEventPayload()`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/LoadGHEventPayload.md)
  : Load the GitHub event payload
- [`LoadUATIssues()`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/LoadUATIssues.md)
  : Load UAT issues from disk
- [`MapIssueClosersToCommits()`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/MapIssueClosersToCommits.md)
  : Add Commits list column to dfIssueClosers
- [`MapLongIssueCommits()`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/MapLongIssueCommits.md)
  **\[experimental\]** : Map issues to commits in long format
- [`MapTestFilesToPotentialIssues()`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/MapTestFilesToPotentialIssues.md)
  **\[experimental\]** : Map test files to potential issues
- [`PrepareTestIssueContext()`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/PrepareTestIssueContext.md)
  **\[experimental\]** : Prepare test issue context for analysis
- [`QCCompletedIssues()`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/QCCompletedIssues.md)
  : Generate a QC report of completed issues
- [`QCIssues()`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/QCIssues.md)
  : Generate a QC report of specific issues
- [`QCMergeGH()`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/QCMergeGH.md)
  : Generate a QC report of issues associated with a GitHub merge
- [`QCMergeLocal()`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/QCMergeLocal.md)
  : Generate a QC report of issues probably related to changes between
  local git refs
- [`QCMilestones()`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/QCMilestones.md)
  : Generate a QC report of a specific milestone or milestones
- [`QCPR()`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/QCPR.md)
  : Generate a QC report of issues associated with a GitHub pull request
- [`QCPackage()`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/QCPackage.md)
  : Generate a QC report for a package
- [`SaveUATIssues()`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/SaveUATIssues.md)
  : Save UAT issues to disk
- [`SetupGHLabels()`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/SetupGHLabels.md)
  : Setup qcthat labels in a GitHub repository
- [`Skill_TagTestsWithIssues()`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/Skill_TagTestsWithIssues.md)
  **\[experimental\]** : Use an AI skill to tag tests with issues
- [`TriggerUAT()`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/TriggerUAT.md)
  **\[experimental\]** : Trigger the UAT cycle for closed issues
- [`print(`*`<qcthat_Object>`*`)`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/printing.md)
  [`format(`*`<qcthat_Object>`*`)`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/printing.md)
  : Printing qcthat objects
- [`use_qcthat()`](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/reference/use_qcthat.md)
  : Set up qcthat for a package

# Articles

### All vignettes

- [Business
  process](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/articles/business_process.md):
- [Setup](https://gilead-biostats.github.io/qcthat/articles/qcthat.md):
